steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Schober's hypothesis says watercourts were designed to foster conch. We need the finding that most directly shows the watercourts could not have served that purpose.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'snails succeeded in watercourts': This would support the hypothesis, not weaken it. ✗
  • Choice B — 'initial resource abundance': Addresses what was available, not whether the design suits conch. ✗
  • Choice C — 'watercourt dimensions suit fish, not shallow seagrass conch require': This shows the physical design itself cannot support the habitat snails need, directly contradicting the hypothesis. ✓
  • Choice D — 'conch are a minority of remains': Shows low frequency but not that the design is unsuitable. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
